skill.name: data-structure-protocol
skill.description: Give agents persistent structural memory of a codebase — navigate dependencies, track public APIs, and understand why connections exist without re-reading the whole repo.
skill.summary: Give AI agents structural memory of codebases via a queryable graph stored in .dsp/ directory
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected patterns in SKILL.md (external_commands, network URLs, weak crypto flags) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The detected patterns are: (1) Example CLI commands in markdown code blocks - not actual shell execution; (2) GitHub reference URLs for documentation - not data exfiltration; (3) Misidentified content as crypto algorithms - the skill is pure documentation. This is a legitimate documentation skill with no security risks.

content.user_title: Map Codebase Structure for AI Agents
content.value_statement: AI agents lose context on large codebases and waste tokens on orientation. This skill provides persistent structural memory through a queryable graph, enabling agents to navigate dependencies and understand code relationships without re-reading files.
actual_capabilities: Initialize a .dsp/ directory to store structural graph data for any codebase
Map entities (objects and functions) with stable UIDs that survive file renames
Record import relationships with 'why' reasons explaining each dependency
Navigate the codebase graph to find affected modules before refactoring
Search entities by name, path, or query to locate specific code components
Perform impact analysis to see which modules depend on a specific library
limitations: Requires manual setup of dsp-cli.py Python script in the project
Does not automatically update the graph - requires explicit agent actions
Does not analyze third-party library internals (only tracks external dependencies)
Agents must remember to update DSP when changing public APIs or imports

use_cases.title: Large Codebase Navigation
use_cases.description: Navigate unfamiliar codebases by querying the DSP graph to understand module relationships without reading every file
use_cases.target_user: Developers working on large monorepos or legacy projects

use_cases.title: Safe Refactoring
use_cases.description: Before replacing a library or refactoring, query the graph to find all dependent modules and understand impact
use_cases.target_user: Engineers performing dependency upgrades or architectural changes

use_cases.title: Code Context Preservation
use_cases.description: Maintain structural knowledge across sessions so AI agents can understand why connections exist
use_cases.target_user: AI coding assistants like Claude, Codex, or Claude Code

prompt_templates.title: Setup DSP for Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the Data Structure Protocol (DSP) for this project. Download dsp-cli.py if needed, run init, and bootstrap the graph starting from the main entrypoint.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new project or enabling DSP on an existing codebase

prompt_templates.title: Find Entity Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all entities that depend on [entity-name-or-path] and show me why each dependency exists.
prompt_templates.scenario: Understanding what will break when changing a module

prompt_templates.title: Map New Module
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new DSP object for [file-path] with description '[purpose]'. Then create function entities for each exported function, mark them as shared, and add imports with reasons.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new file to the codebase

prompt_templates.title: Search Codebase Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the DSP graph for '[query]' to find relevant entities. Show me their descriptions and import relationships.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding code related to a specific feature or concept

output_examples.output:
Found 3 entities:
- obj-11223344: auth/middleware.ts (User authentication middleware)
- func-aabbccdd: auth/middleware.ts#validateToken (JWT token validation)
- obj-44556677: auth/providers/oauth.ts (OAuth provider integration)

Modules importing auth/middleware.ts:
- obj-99887766: src/app.ts ('Handles JWT verification on requests')
- func-12345678: api/routes.ts ('Authenticates incoming API requests')

best_practices: Update DSP immediately when creating new files, adding imports, or changing public APIs
Always add a meaningful 'why' reason when recording an import - this is where most value lives
Treat .dsp/ diffs like code diffs - review them and keep them accurate
anti_patterns: Don't update DSP for internal-only changes that don't affect purpose or dependencies
Don't create UIDs for every local variable - only file-level Objects and public entities
Don't skip adding 'why' reasons - without them the graph loses navigational value

faq.question: What is DSP?
faq.answer: DSP (Data Structure Protocol) is a system that gives AI agents persistent structural memory of a codebase. It stores a queryable graph in a .dsp/ directory showing entities, imports, exports, and reasons for each connection.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides documentation and guidance on using the dsp-cli.py Python script. It does not contain any executable code that runs automatically.

faq.question: How does DSP help with refactoring?
faq.answer: Before making changes, you can query the DSP graph to find all entities that depend on a module and understand WHY they depend on it. This prevents breaking changes and ensures complete refactoring coverage.

faq.question: What are UIDs and why do they matter?
faq.answer: UIDs (unique identifiers like obj-a1b2c3d4) are stable across file renames and moves. Unlike file paths, UIDs allow the graph to track entities consistently even when files are restructured.

faq.question: Does DSP analyze third-party libraries?
faq.answer: No. External dependencies (npm packages, stdlib, etc.) are recorded as 'external' kind but their internals are never analyzed. Only your project's code is mapped in detail.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill is designed for AI coding assistants including Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It helps agents maintain context across sessions without re-reading the entire codebase.
